title: A-File Showdown
setup: White's rook stares up the **a-file** at the black queen and king stacked above her.
hint: When the queen blocks her own king, she is pinned — not skewered. Take her.
explanation:
## The Idea

Despite older lore that calls this a "skewer," this is technically an **absolute pin**: the black queen on a7 shields her king on a8, so she cannot move.

- **Pin** = attacker, target, *king* (king behind).
- **Skewer** = attacker, *king*, target (king in front).

Here the king is behind the queen, so it is a pin.

White plays {{Rxa7+}}, capturing the queen.

- {{Kxa7}} is the only legal reply.
- White trades rook (5) for queen (9), netting **+4**.
- Black has no defender on a7 and no way to interpose.

## Pattern to Memorize

> Geometry first, names second. Whenever you see your **rook + enemy minor/major piece + enemy king** all on the same file, you almost always have a tactic.

Don't worry about whether it's officially a pin or a skewer in the moment — both win material.
